title: Successful and cost-effective DNA extraction  method from insects with small amount of tissue
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"b6rsb","text":"DNA extraction from insects is an initial and critical  step that might affect the molecular taxonomy. There are several protocols for efficient DNA extraction published by researchers, which might be helpful for others working in the relevant field. There are also some  commercial kits optimized to extract DNA even from low volumes or less amount of tissue. Research has conventionally showed that Phenol:  chloroform: Isoamyl alcohol (PCI) method gives good yield and is cost effective as compared to other methodologies, especially commercial kits. Hereby is the CTAB and  PCI protocol, which gives good yield from insect tissue preferably legs,  head and wings (avoid using whole body).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
creator: Rahul Jamdade
